item.text: The first scan found 35 vulnerable packages and the response was truncated, so I’m switching to the bomly CLI JSON output for the complete set. I’ll use the compact scan as the remediation map and the full JSON to avoid missing packages hidden by truncation.

# Top-level build/test tasks.
#
# `make test` rebuilds every fixture from clean installs and runs its test
# suite.
# 
PYTHON ?= python3.12

.PHONY: test test-java clean

test: test-java ## Build + test the fixture from clean

test-java: ## Maven fixture (vendored Dependency-Track 4.10.0): bounded surefire subset
	# -P enhance: DataNucleus JDO bytecode enhancement is a profile upstream,
	# not part of the default lifecycle (see DEVELOPING.md, "DataNucleus
	# Bytecode Enhancement") — without it every @PersistenceCapable-backed
	# test fails with NucleusUserException before ever reaching test logic.
	cd fixtures/api-java && mvn -B -P enhance test -Dtest="org.dependencytrack.model.**,org.dependencytrack.util.**,org.dependencytrack.parser.**" -DfailIfNoTests=false

clean: ## Remove build artifacts and virtualenvs
	rm -rf fixtures/api-java/target
Error: unknown command "fixtures/api-java" for "bomly scan"

item.text: The CLI syntax is different than expected, so I’ll keep using the MCP scanner for authoritative results and use Maven’s resolved dependency tree to verify the actual versions after edits. Next I’m checking the rest of the POM and then I’ll add dependencyManagement pins where the vulnerable libraries are transitive.

        <testResources>
            <testResource>
                <directory>src/test/resources</directory>
                <filtering>false</filtering>
            </testResource>
        </testResources>
        <plugins>
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-checkstyle-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>3.3.1</version>
                <configuration>
                    <configLocation>${project.basedir}/.checkstyle.xml</configLocation>
                    <includeResources>false</includeResources>
                    <includeTestResources>false</includeTestResources>
                </configuration>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <phase>validate</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>check</goal>
                        </goals>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
                <dependencies>
                    <dependency>
                        <groupId>com.puppycrawl.tools</groupId>
                        <artifactId>checkstyle</artifactId>
                        <version>10.12.5</version>
                    </dependency>
                </dependencies>
            </plugin>
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-surefire-plugin</artifactId>
                <dependencies>
                    <dependency>
                        <groupId>org.apache.maven.surefire</groupId>
                        <artifactId>surefire-junit4</artifactId>
                        <version>${maven.surefire.plugin.version}</version>
                    </dependency>
                </dependencies>
            </plugin>
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.codehaus.mojo</groupId>
                <artifactId>exec-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>merge-services-bom</id>
                        <phase>prepare-package</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>exec</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <executable>${cyclonedx-cli.path}</executable>
                            <arguments>
                                <argument>merge</argument>
                                <argument>--input-files</argument>
                                <argument>${project.build.directory}/bom.json</argument>
                                <argument>${project.basedir}/src/main/resources/services.bom.json</argument>
                                <argument>--output-file</argument>
                                <argument>${project.build.directory}/bom.json</argument>
                            </arguments>
                            <skip>${services.bom.merge.skip}</skip>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>
            <plugin>
                <artifactId>maven-antrun-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>3.1.0</version>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>deploy-bom</id>
                        <phase>prepare-package</phase>
                        <configuration>
                            <target>
                                <copy file="${project.build.directory}/bom.json"
                                      tofile="${project.build.directory}/${project.artifactId}/.well-known/sbom"/>
                            </target>
                        </configuration>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>run</goal>
                        </goals>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.eclipse.jetty</groupId>
                <artifactId>jetty-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>${plugin.jetty.version}</version>
                <configuration>
                    <webApp>
                        <contextPath>/</contextPath>
                    </webApp>
                </configuration>
            </plugin>
        </plugins>
    </build>

    <profiles>
        <profile>
            <id>clean-exclude-wars</id>
            <build>
                <pluginManagement>
                    <plugins>
                    <plugin>
                        <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                        <artifactId>maven-clean-plugin</artifactId>
                        <version>3.3.2</version>
                        <configuration>
                            <excludeDefaultDirectories>true</excludeDefaultDirectories>
                            <filesets>
                                <fileset>
                                    <directory>target</directory>
                                    <followSymlinks>false</followSymlinks>
                                    <useDefaultExcludes>false</useDefaultExcludes>
                                    <includes>
                                        <include>*/</include>
                                    </includes>
                                    <excludes>
                                        <exclude>dependency-track*.jar</exclude>
                                    </excludes>
                                </fileset>
                            </filesets>
                        </configuration>
                    </plugin>
                    </plugins>
                </pluginManagement>
            </build>
        </profile>
        <profile>
            <id>embedded-jetty</id>
            <activation>
                <property>
                    <name>env.ACTIVATE_EMBEDDED_JETTY</name>
                    <value>true</value>
                </property>
            </activation>
            <properties>
                <war-embedded-finalname>${project.build.finalName}-apiserver</war-embedded-finalname>
            </properties>
        </profile>
        <profile>
            <id>h2-console</id>
            <build>
                <plugins>
                    <plugin>
                        <groupId>org.eclipse.jetty</groupId>
                        <artifactId>jetty-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                        <version>${plugin.jetty.version}</version>

Scan dependencies and render a graph or SBOM

Usage:
  bomly scan [flags]

Examples:
  bomly scan --enrich --audit
  bomly scan -o spdx=bomly.spdx.json
  bomly scan --url https://github.com/bomly-dev/bomly-cli --ref main --json
  bomly scan --image alpine:3.20

Flags:
      --allow-license stringArray            Allowed SPDX license identifier or expression. Repeatable
      --allow-vulnerability-id stringArray   Vulnerability ID to ignore during policy evaluation. Repeatable
      --analyze                              [Experimental] Run code analysis to confirm whether vulnerabilities are reachable from application code
      --analyzers string                     Reachability analyzer selectors. Use names. Prefix with '+' to append defaults or '-' to remove from defaults
      --audit                                Evaluate policy and create findings from package vulnerability data
      --auditors string                      Auditor selectors. Use names or aliases. Prefix with '+' to append defaults or '-' to remove from defaults
      --deny-group stringArray               Package URL namespace to deny. Repeatable
      --deny-license stringArray             Denied SPDX license identifier or expression. Repeatable
      --deny-package stringArray             Package URL to deny. Repeatable
      --detectors string                     Detector selectors. Use names or aliases. Prefix with '+' to append defaults or '-' to remove from defaults
      --ecosystems string                    Ecosystems to use; supports +name/-name to add/remove from all
      --enrich                               Enrich packages with external license and vulnerability data
      --fail-on stringArray                  Constraint(s) for which findings should be created. Repeatable; constraints AND together. Severity: any|low|medium|high|critical. Reachability: reachable. Exploitability: exploitable. Example: --fail-on low --fail-on reachable
  -f, --format string                        Output format: text, json, markdown, sarif, spdx, cyclonedx (SBOM formats are scan-only)
  -h, --help                                 help for scan
      --image string                         Container image reference to scan with Syft
      --install-arg stringArray              Additional detector-specific install argument; may be repeated
      --install-first                        Run detector-specific dependency installation before resolving graphs
      --interactive                          Open an interactive terminal UI
      --json                                 Shortcut for --format json
      --license-exempt-package stringArray   Package URL exempt from license policy checks. Repeatable
      --matchers string                      Matcher selectors. Use names or aliases. Prefix with '+' to append defaults or '-' to remove from defaults
  -o, --output stringArray                   Additional output target as <format> or <format>=<path>; repeat for multiple outputs. Supports text, json, markdown, sarif, spdx, cyclonedx
      --path string                          Execution target path
      --protected-package stringArray        Canonical package name to protect from typosquatting. Repeatable
      --ref string                           Git reference to scan when using --url
      --sbom                                 Treat the selected filesystem target as an SBOM file
      --scope string                         Filter dependencies by scope: runtime or development
      --typosquat-mode string                Typosquatting policy mode: warn or fail
      --typosquat-threshold string           Similarity threshold for typosquatting detection
      --url string                           Git repository URL to clone and scan
      --warn-only                            Downgrade failing findings to warnings

Global Flags:
      --config string   YAML config file path
  -q, --quiet           Suppress non-error stderr output
  -v, --verbose count   Increase verbosity (-v = info, -vv = debug)

Explore available detectors, matchers, and auditors with `bomly plugins list`.

item.text: I found the right CLI flag (`--path`). I’ll generate the full scan file now, then extract the unique vulnerability packages and highest fixed versions so the Maven edits line up with the scanner’s data.

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
import json
from collections import defaultdict
p='/tmp/bomly-api-java-scan.json'
data=json.load(open(p))
findings=data.get('findings') or data.get('audit_findings') or []
print(data.keys())
print('findings', len(findings))
by=defaultdict(list)
for f in findings:
    if f.get('kind')=='vulnerability':
        pkg=f.get('package',{})
        by[(pkg.get('name'), pkg.get('version'), pkg.get('purl'))].append(f)
print('vuln packages', len(by))
for (name,ver,purl),fs in sorted(by.items()):
    ids=[]
    fixed=set()
    sev=[]
    for f in fs:
        ids.append(f.get('id') or f.get('vuln_id'))
        sev.append(f.get('severity'))
        if f.get('fixed_in'): fixed.add(f.get('fixed_in'))
        for a in f.get('advisories',[]) or []:
            if a.get('fixed_in'): fixed.add(str(a.get('fixed_in')))
    print(f'{name}@{ver} count={len(fs)} sev={sorted(set(sev))} fixed={sorted(fixed)} ids={\",\".join(ids)}')
PY"

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
import json,re
from collections import defaultdict
sev_order={'low':1,'medium':2,'high':3,'critical':4}
data=json.load(open('/tmp/bomly-api-java-scan.json'))
by=defaultdict(lambda:{'ids':[],'versions':set(),'fixes':set(),'titles':[],'sevs':set()})
for f in data['findings']:
    if f.get('kind')"'!='"'vulnerability': continue
    pkg=f['package']; key=(pkg['name'],pkg['version'])
    d=by[key]; d['ids'].append(f['id']); d['titles'].append(f['title']); d['sevs'].add(f['severity'])
    for r in f.get('reasons',[]):
        m=re.search(r'Fix available: upgrade to\\s+(["'^;'"\\s]+)', r)
        if m: d['fixes'].add(m.group(1))
for (name,ver),d in sorted(by.items()):
    print(f'{name}@{ver}')
    print('  fixes:', ', '.join(sorted(d['fixes'])) or 'NONE')
    print('  ids:', ', '.join(d['ids']))
    print('  titles:', ' | '.join(d['titles'][:4]) + (' ...' if len(d['titles'])>4 else ''))
PY"

item.text: There are 35 vulnerable Maven packages. Several are test-only transitives from WireMock/MockServer; others come through Alpine or direct app dependencies. I’ll use dependencyManagement pins plus direct property bumps, which is the least invasive way to remediate without refactoring code.

item.text: I’m editing `pom.xml` now: direct vulnerable dependencies get version bumps, MySQL moves to the current `com.mysql:mysql-connector-j` coordinate because the old coordinate has no `8.2.0` artifact, and transitive vulnerable libraries get dependencyManagement pins.
The POM edits are in place. I’m resolving the vulnerable package set again first; if Maven reports dependency conflicts or missing artifacts, I’ll correct those before running the full test target.

item.text: The rescan reduced fixable findings, but two more decisions surfaced: `cyclonedx-core-java` needs `11.0.1`, and Jetty 9.4 still has a fixable `jetty-http` advisory whose patched line is Jetty 12. I’m checking whether the test dependency can move to WireMock 3.x cleanly instead of forcing an incompatible single Jetty 12 module under WireMock 2.x.

item.text: WireMock usage is test-only and sticks to the public `com.github.tomakehurst.wiremock` API that WireMock 3 still provides. I’m going to move the test dependency to `org.wiremock:wiremock:3.13.1` and bump CycloneDX to the scanner’s fixed version, then compile/tests will tell us if any API incompatibility matters.
The follow-up edits are in place. I’m running the repo’s required `make test` now; this is the real compatibility check for the dependency upgrades.

item.text: `make test` failed at compile because CycloneDX 11 removed or moved several APIs used by the app. Since that bump is required for the remaining fixable CycloneDX advisory, I’m making the smallest source updates needed for the new API and leaving unrelated code untouched.

item.text: The CycloneDX API changes are small: factory classes moved packages, and license expressions are now wrapped in an `Expression` type. I’m checking the exact constructor/method signatures before patching those three files.
I have the signatures now. I’m applying a narrow compatibility patch: package imports and converting CycloneDX license `Expression` objects to/from their string value at the existing boundaries.
The CycloneDX compatibility patch is applied. I’m rerunning `make test` to catch the next real issue, if any.

item.text: The final scan showed my old Jetty 9 dependencyManagement pins were still overriding WireMock 3’s newer Jetty line. I’m removing those stale Jetty pins so the WireMock upgrade can actually take effect, then I’ll rescan.

item.text: With the stale pins removed, WireMock is on Jetty 11.0.24. Bomly still has two fixable Jetty findings: HTTP/2 common has a same-major fix at `11.0.26`, while `jetty-http` only shows a Jetty 12 fix. I’m checking whether a newer WireMock release already moves those transitives before deciding what can be safely fixed.

item.text: WireMock has a stable `3.13.2` and 4.x betas. I’ll try the stable patch release first; using a beta would be a much larger risk unless it is the only viable fixed path.

item.text: WireMock `3.13.2` only gets Jetty to 11.0.26; it cannot clear the `jetty-http` finding that bomly says is fixed in Jetty 12. I’m trying WireMock `4.0.0-beta.38` because it brings Jetty 12.1.10. If it builds and tests pass, that is the complete version-based remediation path for the test dependency.
The WireMock 4 beta and Handlebars patch bump are in the POM. I’m running `make test` again before trusting the scan result.

item.text: WireMock 4 removes the JUnit 4 rule API this codebase uses, so that upgrade is not compatible without rewriting unrelated tests. I’m backing down to the stable WireMock 3.13.2 path, which fixes the same-major Jetty HTTP/2 issue; I’ll document the remaining Jetty 12-only findings explicitly.

item.text: There is a better option than WireMock 4: WireMock publishes `wiremock-jetty12` on the stable 3.x line. I’m switching to `org.wiremock:wiremock-jetty12:3.13.2`; it should keep the 3.x API while using Jetty 12.

item.text: The `wiremock-jetty12` POM keeps `org.wiremock:wiremock:3.13.2` but excludes its Jetty 11 modules and supplies Jetty 12.0.30. I’m applying that dependency swap and testing again.
The stable Jetty 12 WireMock artifact is now selected. I’m rerunning `make test`; if it passes, this should leave only advisories that bomly classifies as no-fix upstream.
The Jetty 12 WireMock build is compiling and downloading its dependency set. I’m waiting for the final result.
The stable Jetty 12 WireMock path compiled and the test subset is running. I’m waiting for completion and then I’ll do the final vulnerability scan.

item.text: The Jetty 12 artifact exposed newer Jetty advisories fixed in 12.0.32/12.0.33. I’m going to import the Jetty 12.0.33 BOMs in our dependencyManagement so WireMock’s Jetty 12 modules resolve to the patched line consistently.
The Jetty 12.0.33 BOMs and HTTP/2 artifact resolve from Maven Central. I’m adding the BOM imports at the top of dependencyManagement, then I’ll rerun tests and scan.
The Jetty BOM imports are in place. I’m running `make test` once more before the final scan and fix log.
